revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:長福寺 (美作市)]][[ru:Тёфукудзи]][[uk:Тьофукудзі (Мімасака)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Makisan Chofukuji Pagoda.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Mimasaka|qid=Q861279|1=Mimasaka, Okayama}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.926178|N|134.150506|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
{{Temple | Name = Chōfuku-ji | image = [[File:Makisan Chofukuji Pagoda.jpg|240px]] | image_caption = The three-storied pagoda (Important Cultural Property) | Location = 414 Magami, Mimasaka, Okayama Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|55|36.0|N|134|9|1.8|E|scale:20000_region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|qq=}} | Mountain_name = [[Maki Mountain]] (真木山) | Institute_name = [[Hannya-in]] | Temple_name = Chōfuku-ji | Denomination = [[Kogi Shingon Buddhism]] | Sect = [[Shingon Omuro Sect]] | Main_image = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|qid=Q11404731|lt=Ekādaśamukha|1=Ekādaśamukha}} (Important Cultural Property) | Founded = {{Heisei Japanese era|qq=}} (trad. [[:en:757 AD|757 AD]]) | Founder = (trad.) [[:en:Ganjin|Ganjin]]; {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|qid=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken|1=Empress Kōken}} (Imperial edict) | Restored = [[Kōan]] 8 (1285) | Restorer = {{ill|Engen|en|Engen|qid=Q1194159|lt=Engen|1=Engen}} | Formal_name = Maki Mountain Hannya-in Chōfuku-ji | Affiliation = [[Mimasaka Saigoku 33 Kannon]] Pilgrimage (2nd site), [[Harima-Mimasaka Seven Lucky Gods]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) | Cultural_properties = Three-storied pagoda, Eleven-Faced Kannon statue, Colored scrolls including Fudō Myōō, Ryōkai Mandala, Twelve Heavenly Deities (Important Cultural Properties) }} '''Chōfuku-ji''' (長福寺) is a [[Shingon Omuro Sect]] Buddhist temple in [[Magami, Mimasaka, Okayama Prefecture]]. The temple, formally known as Maki Mountain Hannya-in Chōfuku-ji, houses the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|qid=Q11404731|lt=Ekādaśamukha|1=Ekādaśamukha}} as its principal image. Its three-storied pagoda, designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}, is the oldest wooden structure in Okayama Prefecture. == History == According to temple tradition, Chōfuku-ji was founded in [[:en:757 AD|757 AD]] during the {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|qid=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} era by [[:en:Ganjin|Ganjin]] under an imperial edict issued by {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|qid=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken|1=Empress Kōken}}. However, historical verification of this account is lacking, and the temple's early history remains unclear. Initially located on the summit of [[Maki Mountain]], it was later moved to its current location at the base of the mountain. The temple experienced decline until it was restored in the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, specifically in {{ill|1285|en|1285|qid=Q5546|lt=1285|1=1285}} by the monk {{ill|Engen|en|Engen|qid=Q1194159|lt=Engen|1=Engen}} as a [[:en:Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] temple. At its peak, the temple housed 65 monk quarters. Over time, it oscillated between the Tendai and {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon|1=Shingon Buddhism}} sects before permanently affiliating with Shingon Buddhism during the {{ill|Meitoku|en|Meitoku|qid=Q1145032|lt=Meitoku|1=Meitoku}} era (1390–1393). During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the temple saw a decline from 40 monk quarters in the early period to 25 in the mid-period, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was reduced to only four associated temples. A fire in {{ill|1876|en|1876|qid=Q7766|lt=1876|1=1876}} destroyed much of the complex, leaving only the Okunoin intact. The temple was relocated to its current site in {{ill|1928|en|1928|qid=Q19715|lt=1928|1=1928}} and rebuilt, including the relocation of its three-storied pagoda and associated structures. == Cultural Properties == ; Important Cultural Properties (National) * **Three-Storied Pagoda** - Standing 22.07 meters tall, this structure was built in 1285 by master carpenter Fujiwara Kuniyemon-no-jō. It underwent significant repairs during the [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] period (1624–1643), in {{ill|1823|en|1823|qid=Q6988|lt=1823|1=1823}}, and in the late Meiji era. Designated as an Important Cultural Property in {{ill|1921|en|1921|qid=Q2162|lt=1921|1=1921}} under the [[Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act]] and reaffirmed in {{ill|1950|en|1950|qid=Q18597|lt=1950|1=1950}} under the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law]], it was relocated to the current site in 1951 and underwent preservation work in {{ill|1980|en|1980|qid=Q2439|lt=1980|1=1980}}. * **Wooden Eleven-Faced Kannon Statue** - Standing 258 cm tall, this statue, attributed to the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} or early {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, is constructed of cypress wood using the joined-block technique. Traditionally ascribed to {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|qid=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}}, it was designated an Important Cultural Property in {{ill|1901|en|1901|qid=Q2035|lt=1901|1=1901}}. * **Colored Scroll of Fudō Myōō** - Measuring 134 x 74 cm, this mid-Kamakura period scroll was designated in 1901. * **Ryōkai Mandala Scrolls** - Two scrolls from the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|qid=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}} measuring 165 x 125 cm each. Designated in 1901. * **Twelve Heavenly Deities Scrolls** - A set of 12 scrolls depicting deities such as {{ill|Brahma|en|Brahma|qid=Q11389|lt=Brahma|1=Brahma}} and {{ill|Indra|en|Indra|qid=Q128335|lt=Indra|1=Indra}}, attributed to monk Zōun during the Nanboku-chō period. Designated in 1901. == Annual Festivals == * **Kokūzō Festival** (十三参り): Held on January 15 annually, the festival celebrates children's health and academic success. == Access == * **Train**: Take the {{ill|JR|en|JR|qid=Q127942|lt=JR|1=JR}} {{ill|Kishin Line|en|Kishin Line|qid=Q869221|lt=Kishin Line|1=Kishin Line}} to [[Hayashino Station]], then transfer to a [[Uno Bus]] bound for {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|qid=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station|1=Okayama Station}}. Alight at Fukumoto Bus Stop, and walk 30 minutes to the temple. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=133 Chōfuku-ji Information] - AT Web Magazine {{Commonscat-inline|qq=}}
